description: The PCGL Data Dictionary specifies the standardized data elements, controlled vocabulary, data types, validation rules, and interoperability mappings for all health and genomic data submitted to the Pan-Canadian Genome Library.
name: Pan-Canadian Genome Library Data Dictionary

schemas.description: Study refers to a specific research project conducted as part of a larger program. It involves data collection, analysis, and interpretation related to the program's goals. Each study is focused on particular research questions related to set of objectives. Upon registration will be provided a PCGL ID following the regex convention: `PCGLST[0-9]{4}`

fields.description: The official name of the study
fields.name: study_name

fields.description: A detailed description of the study's purpose, hypothesis, and design.
fields.name: study_description

fields.description: Indicate the overarching program the study belongs to (if applicable)
fields.name: program_name

fields.description: List of specific terms that describe the focus and content of the study. In case there are several, place each on separate line.
fields.name: keywords

fields.description: Indicate if the study is completed or ongoing.
fields.name: status

fields.description: Indicate if the study was conducted in a clinical setting or as part of a research project.
fields.name: context

fields.description: List of specific scientific or clinical domains addressed by the study. In case there are several, place each on separate line.
fields.name: domain

fields.description: Unique identifier of the Data Access Committee (DAC) in PCGL to which the study is assigned. This will be supplied by PCGL Admin
fields.name: dac_id

fields.description: Inclusion/exclusion criteria for participants (e.g., specific cancer type, age range).
fields.name: participant_criteria

fields.description:
List of lead researchers responsible for the study.
In case there are several, please list each investigator on a separate line in the format:
FirstName LastName, Affiliation
fields.name: principal_investigators

fields.description:
List of institutions or organizations leading the study.
In case there are several, please list each lead organization on a separate line using its full official name.
fields.name: lead_organizations

fields.description:
List of researchers, institutions or companies involved in the study.

In case there are several, please list each collaborator on a separate line in the format:
FirstName LastName (if individual) or Organization full official name, Role
fields.name: collaborators

fields.description:
List of organizations or agencies funding the study. 

In case there are several, please list each funding source on a separate line in the format:
Funder name, Grant number
fields.name: funding_sources

fields.description:
List of URL links to academic papers or reports associated with the study. 
In case there are several, please list each publication on a separate line by providing a DOI URL formatted as https://doi.org/ followed by the DOI number.
fields.name: publication_links

schemas.name: study

schemas.description: A group of individuals responsible for reviewing and approving or denying access requests to data based on established policies. Upon registration will be provided a PCGL ID following the regex convention: `PCGLDA[0-9]{4}`

fields.description: Full name of the DAC.
fields.name: dac_name

fields.description: An in-depth description of the DAC, including its overall purpose, scope and responsibilities.
fields.name: dac_description

fields.description: The full name of the primary contact person. Please provide in the format: FirstName LastName
fields.name: contact_name

fields.description: Contact email for communication.
fields.name: contact_email

schemas.name: dac

schemas.description: The collection of all data related to a specific individual human in the context of a specific study.

fields.description: Unique identifier of the participant within the study, assigned by the data provider.
fields.name: submitter_participant_id

fields.description: Select one of the Data Use Ontology (DUO) codes in Compact URI (CURIE) pattern to indicate permitted uses or focused areas of research for the Participant data.
fields.name: duo_permission

codeList: DUO:0000006 (health or medical or biomedical research)
DUO:0000011 (population origins or ancestry research only)

fields.description: Collection of Data Use Ontology (DUO) codes in Compact URI (CURIE) pattern to add requirements, limitations, or prohibitions within the permitted boundary for the Participant data.
fields.name: duo_modifier

codeList: DUO:0000018 (not for profit, non commercial use only)
DUO:0000045 (not for profit organisation use only)
DUO:00000044 (population origins or ancestry research prohibited)

fields.description: If the permission is DUO:0000007 (disease specific research), then MONDO codes must be provided to specify which disease(s) apply.
fields.name: disease_specific_modifier

schemas.name: participant

schemas.description: Captures sociodemographic characteristics of an individual that may vary over time and can influence health status, access to care, or disease outcomes. These factors are often collected longitudinally and reflect broader social determinants of health. The PCGL requires information on eight key sociodemographic variables defined by the Canadian Institute for Health Information (CIHI).

fields.description: Unique identifier for the sociodemographic record, assigned by the data provider.
fields.name: submitter_sociodem_id

fields.description: Unique identifier of the participant within the study, assigned by the data provider.
fields.name: submitter_participant_id

fields.description: Age (in days) of the participant at the time of sociodemographic data collection
fields.name: age_at_sociodem_collection

fields.description:
Many sociodemographic variables are social constructs, and the way participants self-identify may evolve over time. 
Please indicate the actual date when the data was collected in format: YYYY-MM-DD, if available. 
If the exact day is unknown, partial dates are acceptable:
- Use YYYY-MM if the day is unknown but the month and year are known
- Use YYYY if only the year is known
fields.name: sociodem_date_collection

fields.description:
Race is a social construct used to judge and categorize people based on perceived differences in physical appearance in ways that create and maintain power differentials within social hierarchies.
There is no scientifically supported biological basis for discrete racial groups
[REF: Canadian Institute for Health Information.Guidance on the Use of Standards for Race-Based and Indigenous Identity Data Collection and Health Reporting in Canada. (2022)](https://www.cihi.ca/sites/default/files/document/guidance-and-standards-for-race-based-and-indigenous-identity-data-en.pdf).
Participants may have multiple racial identities.
All categories selected by the participants should be reported.
fields.name: race

fields.description: A response which does not fall into any of the previous racial reporting categories (e.g., Pacific Islander, Indigenous identities outside of North America, etc.).
fields.name: race_another_racial_category

fields.description:
Studies must report how race data was collected
. This is necessary to enable data aggregation, harmonization, and reproducibility.
Responses from prospective projects should always be self-identified.
To respect participants' autonomy and right to self-determination, as well as to avoid conflating differences in (genetic) population structure with socially constructed racial categories,responses that were self-identified should not be aggregated with responses that were socially assigned or derived.
fields.name: race_collect_method

fields.description:
Gender refers to the socially constructed roles, behaviours, expressions and identities of girls, women, boys, men, and gender diverse people.
A person’s gender may be the same as or different from their assigned sex at birth and may change over time.
fields.name: gender

fields.description: A response which does not fall into any of the previous gender reporting categories.
fields.name: gender_another_gender

fields.description: Studies must report how the gender data was collected. This is necessary to enable data aggregation, harmonization, and reproducibility.
fields.name: gender_collect_method

fields.description:
Ethnicity is a 'multi-dimensional concept referring to community belonging and a shared cultural group membership.
It is related to sociodemographic characteristics, including language, religion, geographic origin, nationality, cultural traditions, ancestry and migration history, among others,'
[REF: Canadian Institute for Health Information. Guidance on the Use of Standards for Race-Based and Indigenous Identity Data Collection and Health Reporting in Canada (2022)][REF: Canadian Institute for Health Information.Guidance on the Use of Standards for Race-Based and Indigenous Identity Data Collection and Health Reporting in Canada.(2022)](https://www.cihi.ca/sites/default/files/document/guidance-and-standards-for-race-based-and-indigenous-identity-data-en.pdf).
Participants may have multiple ethnic and/or cultural identities.
All categories entered by the participants should be reported.
fields.name: ethnicity

fields.description: A response which does not fall into any of the previous categories. For example, a response of “Other” from retrospective studies. Prospective projects should instead allow participants to freely self-identify.
fields.name: ethnicity_another_category

fields.description: Studies must report how the ethnicity data was collected. This is necessary to enable data aggregation, harmonization, and reproducibility.
fields.name: ethnicity_collect_method

fields.description: Sex is a biological concept that has been historically defined solely based on external anatomy; however, sex is also influenced by our physiology, genes, and hormones.
fields.name: sex_at_birth

fields.description: A response which does not fall into any of the previous categories.
fields.name: sex_another_category

fields.description: Studies must report how the sex_at_birth data was collected. This is necessary to enable data aggregation, harmonization, and reproducibility.
fields.name: sex_collect_method

fields.description: The highest level of education attained.
fields.name: education

codeList: High school (secondary school) diploma or equivalency certificate
Certificate of Apprenticeship, Certificate of Qualification (Journeyperson's designation), or other trades certificate or diploma
College, CEGEP, or other non-university certificate or diploma
Degree in medicine, dentistry, veterinary medicine or optometry

fields.description: Studies must report how the education data was collected. This is necessary to enable data aggregation, harmonization, and reproducibility.
fields.name: education_collect_method

fields.description: Personal annual income from all sources after taxes.
fields.name: personal_income

fields.description: Studies must report how the personal_income data was collected. This is necessary to enable data aggregation, harmonization, and reproducibility.
fields.name: personal_income_collect_method

fields.description: A participant's postal code at their current address of residence.
fields.name: geographic_location

restrictions.regex: ^[A-Za-z][0-9][A-Za-z][0-9][A-Za-z][0-9]$|^Missing \- Unknown$|^Missing \- Not collected$|^Missing \- Not provided$|^Missing \- Restricted access$

fields.description: When postal code is less specific, additional information may be reported.
fields.name: geographic_location_additional

fields.description: Studies must report how the geographic_location data was collected. This is necessary to enable data aggregation, harmonization, and reproducibility.
fields.name: geographic_location_collect_method

fields.description: Indicate whether the questions asked for the sociodemographic variable follow the PCGL reference question and standard reporting categories.
fields.name: sociodem_question

fields.description: Report the questions asked for the sociodemographic variable, including the provided response options/reporting categories if it does not follow the PCGL reference question and standard reporting categories.
fields.name: sociodem_question_detail

fields.description: Studies may choose to report whether potential bias may exist due to the way reporting categories were defined and handled.

examples:
For example, in the Canadian Census, the order of the provided response options for ethnicity were shown to influence number of received responses per reporting option. 

Other examples may include inclusion/exclusion criteria based on sociodemographic variables.

fields.name: sociodem_notes

schemas.name: sociodemographic

schemas.description: Data for the characterization of the participant by means of segmenting the population (e.g., characterization by age, sex, or race).

fields.description: Unique identifier of the participant within the study, assigned by the data provider.
fields.name: submitter_participant_id

fields.description: Age (in days) of participant at time of enrollment into the study
fields.name: age_at_enrollment

fields.description: Participant's last known state of living or deceased.
fields.name: vital_status

fields.description: Indicate the cause of a participant's death.
fields.name: cause_of_death

fields.description: Age of participant (in days) at time of death
fields.name: age_at_death

schemas.name: demographic

schemas.description: The disease that is inferred to be present in the individual, family or cohort being analyzed.

fields.description: Unique identifier of the participant within the study, assigned by the data provider.
fields.name: submitter_participant_id

fields.description: Unique identifier of the primary diagnosis event, assigned by the data provider.
fields.name: submitter_diagnosis_id

fields.description: Age of participant (in days) at time of diagnosis of the condition.
fields.name: age_at_diagnosis

fields.description: Use ICD-10 code or MONDO code to represent the disease diagnosed. Provide code in Compact URI (CURIE) pattern.

required: Provide code in Compact URI (CURIE) pattern. ICD-10 code: refer to https://icd.who.int/browse10/2019/en MONDO code: refer to https://www.ebi.ac.uk/ols/ontologies/mondo

fields.name: disease_code

fields.description: Provide the standardized and human readable term derived from the coding system associated with the disease_code
fields.name: disease_term

schemas.name: diagnosis

schemas.description: Medications, procedures, other actions taken for clinical management

fields.description: Unique identifier of the participant within the study, assigned by the data provider.
fields.name: submitter_participant_id

fields.description: Unique identifier of the treatment, assigned by the data provider.
fields.name: submitter_treatment_id

fields.description: The category or method of treatment administered
fields.name: treatment_type

fields.description: Unique identifier of the primary diagnosis event, assigned by the data provider.
fields.name: submitter_diagnosis_id

fields.description: Age (in days) of the participant at the time the treatment was administered.
fields.name: age_at_treatment

fields.description: The length of time (in days) over which the treatment was administered.
fields.name: treatment_duration

fields.description: The purpose of the treatment or the desired effect or outcome resulting from the treatment.
fields.name: treatment_intent

fields.description: The outcome of the treatment, indicating how the patient responded to the intervention
fields.name: treatment_response

fields.description: Indicate the participant's status of the prescribed treatment.
fields.name: treatment_status

codeList: Treatment incomplete due to technical or organizational problems
Treatment stopped due to acute toxicity
Treatment stopped due to lack of efficacy (disease progression)

schemas.name: treatment

schemas.description: Any point of contact with a patient after primary diagnosis.

fields.description: Unique identifier of the participant within the study, assigned by the data provider.
fields.name: submitter_participant_id

fields.description: Participant's age (in days) at time of the follow up event
fields.name: age_at_followup

fields.description: Indicate the participant's disease status at time of follow-up
fields.name: disease_status_at_followup

schemas.name: follow_up

schemas.description: A clinical procedure performed on a subject. For example a surgical or diagnostic procedure such as a biopsy.

fields.description: Unique identifier of the treatment, assigned by the data provider.
fields.name: submitter_treatment_id

required:
Provide code in Compact URI (CURIE) pattern.  NCIt URL: https://ncit.nci.nih.gov/ncitbrowser/ConceptReport.jsp?dictionary=NCI_Thesaurus&code=C<code>
  SNOMED-CT URL: http://snomed.info/id/<code>
  UMLS URL: https://uts.nlm.nih.gov/uts/umls/concept/<code> CCI URL:https://www.cihi.ca/en/cci-coding-structure

fields.name: procedure_code

fields.description: Provide the standardized and human readable term derived from the coding system associated with the procedure_code
fields.name: procedure_term

fields.description:
Indicate the ICD-O-3 topography code or UBERON code for the anatomic location of a specimen when it was collected.
Please use C80.9 if the anatomic location of a specimen is Unknown.
Provide code in Compact URI (CURIE) pattern.
Refer to the International Classification of Diseases for Oncology, 3rd Edition (WHO ICD-O-3) manual for guidelines at 
https://apps.who.int/iris/handle/10665/42344
UBERON URL:
http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_{code}

required: Refer to the International Classification of Diseases for Oncology, 3rd Edition (WHO ICD-O-3) manual for guidelines at https://apps.who.int/iris/handle/10665/42344

fields.name: procedure_body_site_code

fields.description: Provide the standardized and human readable term derived from the coding system associated with the procedure_body_site_code
fields.name: procedure_body_site_term

schemas.name: procedure

schemas.description: An agent such as a drug (pharmaceutical agent), broadly defined as prescription and over-the-counter medicines, vaccines, and large-molecule biologic therapies.

fields.description: Unique identifier of the treatment, assigned by the data provider.
fields.name: submitter_treatment_id

required: Provide code in Compact URI (CURIE) pattern.  RxNorm URL: https://rxnav.nlm.nih.gov/REST/rxcui/{code}  KEGG URL: https://www.kegg.jp/entry/{code}  PubChem URL: https://pubchem.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/compound/{code}  NCIt URL: https://ncit.nci.nih.gov/ncitbrowser/ConceptReport.jsp?dictionary=NCI_Thesaurus&code=C{code}

fields.name: drug_code

fields.description: Provide the standardized and human readable term derived from the coding system associated with the drug_code
fields.name: drug_term

fields.description: Provide the ontology of standardized units to indicate units used to record drug dose.
fields.name: drug_dose_units

fields.description: Indicate the total prescribed cumulative drug dose in the same units specified in drug_dose_units.
fields.name: prescribed_cumulative_drug_dose

fields.description: Indicate the total actual cumulative drug dose in the same units specified in drug_dose_units.
fields.name: actual_cumulative_drug_dose

schemas.name: medication

schemas.description: Uses ionizing radiation, generally as part of cancer treatment to control or kill malignant cells.

fields.description: Unique identifier of the treatment, assigned by the data provider.
fields.name: submitter_treatment_id

fields.description:
Indicate NCIt code to denote the modality of radiation therapy.
Provide code in Compact URI (CURIE) pattern.
fields.name: radiation_modality_code

fields.description: Provide the standardized and human readable term derived from the coding system associated with the radiation modality code.
fields.name: radiation_modality_term

fields.description: Indicate the total number of fractions delivered as part of treatment.
fields.name: radiation_fractions

fields.description: Indicate the total dose given in units of Gray (Gy).
fields.name: radiation_dosage

fields.description:
Indicate the ICD-O-3 topography code or UBERON code for the anatomic location where radiation therapy was administered.
 Please use C80.9 if the anatomic location is Unkown. Provide code in Compact URI (CURIE) pattern.

 Refer to the International Classification of Diseases for Oncology, 3rd Edition (WHO ICD-O-3) manual for guidelines at https://apps.who.int/iris/handle/10665/42344

 UBERON URL: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_{code}

required: Refer to the International Classification of Diseases for Oncology, 3rd Edition (WHO ICD-O-3) manual for guidelines at https://apps.who.int/iris/handle/10665/42344

fields.name: anatomical_site_irradiated_code

fields.description: Provide the standardized and human readable term derived from the coding system associated with the anatomical_site_irradiated_code
fields.name: anatomical_site_irradiated_term

schemas.name: radiation

schemas.description: Record individual measurements to capture quantitative, ordinal (e.g., absent/present), or categorical measurements.

fields.description: Unique identifier of the participant within the study, assigned by the data provider.
fields.name: submitter_participant_id

fields.description: Age (in days) of the participant at the time the lab test or measurement was conducted.
fields.name: age_at_measurement

fields.description:
Use standardized LOINC (Logical Observation Identifiers Names and Codes) code to represent quantitative, ordinal, or categorical measurements.
Provide code in Compact URI (CURIE) pattern.

required: Provide code in Compact URI (CURIE) pattern. LOINC URL: https://loinc.org/{code}

fields.name: measurement_code

fields.description: Provide the standardized and human readable term derived from the coding system associated with the measurement_code
fields.name: measurement_term

fields.description: The numeric result of the lab test or measurement.
fields.name: measurement_result_numeric

fields.description: Provide the ontology of standardized units to indicate units used to record measurement result. Provide code in Compact URI (CURIE) pattern. UO URL: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UO_{code}
fields.name: measurement_unit

fields.description: The categorical result of the lab test or measurement.
fields.name: measurement_result_categorical

schemas.name: measurement

schemas.description: Individual phenotypic features, observed as either present or absent (excluded), with possible onset, modifiers and frequency

fields.description: Unique identifier of the participant within the study, assigned by the data provider.
fields.name: submitter_participant_id

fields.description: Participant's age (in days) when phenotype was observed
fields.name: age_at_phenotype

fields.description:
Use standardized HPO (Human Phenotype Ontology) codes to represent the phenotype.
Provide code in Compact URI (CURIE) pattern.

required: Provide code in Compact URI (CURIE) pattern.  HPO URL: https://hpo.jax.org/app/browse/term/HP:<code>

fields.name: phenotype_code

fields.description: Provide the standardized and human readable term derived from the coding system associated with the phenotype_code.
fields.name: phenotype_term

fields.description: Indicate whether the phenotype was observed in the participant.
fields.name: phenotype_observed

fields.description: Indicate the length of time (in days) over which the phenotype was observed in the participant.
fields.name: phenotype_duration

fields.description: The degree or severity of the observed phenotype.
fields.name: phenotype_severity

schemas.name: phenotype

schemas.description: Any medical conditions (e.g diabetes, prior cancer malignancies) that have existed or may occur during the clinical course of the participant who has the index disease under study.

fields.description: Unique identifier of the participant within the study, assigned by the data provider.
fields.name: submitter_participant_id

fields.description: Indicate the age (in days) of comorbidity diagnosis.

fields.description: Use ICD-10 code or Mondo code to indicate the comorbidity diagnosed. Provide code in Compact URI (CURIE) pattern.

required: Provide code in Compact URI (CURIE) pattern. ICD-10 code: refer to https://icd.who.int/browse10/2019/en MONDO code: refer to https://www.ebi.ac.uk/ols/ontologies/mondo

fields.name: comorbidity_code

fields.description: Provide the standardized and human readable term derived from the coding system associated with the comorbidity_code
fields.name: comorbidity_term

fields.description: Indicate whether the comorbid condition is currently being treated or not.
fields.name: comorbidity_treatment_status

fields.description: Indicate the current state or activity of the comorbid condition.
fields.name: comorbidity_status

schemas.name: comorbidity

schemas.description: Capture information about external factors, agents, or conditions an individual has encountered that may influence health or disease outcomes.

fields.description: Unique identifier of the participant within the study, assigned by the data provider.
fields.name: submitter_participant_id

fields.description:
Provide the standardized codes from SNOMED CT, ExO, LOINC to represent the type of exposure.
Provide code in Compact URI (CURIE) pattern.
fields.name: exposure_code

fields.description: Provide the standardized and human readable term derived from the coding system associated with the exposure_code
fields.name: exposure_term

fields.description: Provide the status of the exposure
fields.name: exposure_status

fields.description: Age (in days) when the participant was exposed (or first exposed)
fields.name: age_at_exposure

fields.description: Duration of exposure period in days
fields.name: exposure_duration

fields.description: Provide intensity, quantity or frequency of the exposure
fields.name: exposure_amount

fields.description: Unit associated with exposure_amount
fields.name: exposure_unit

schemas.name: exposure

schemas.description: Any material sample taken from a biological entity, living or dead, from physical object or the environment

fields.description: Unique identifier of the participant within the study, assigned by the data provider.
fields.name: submitter_participant_id

fields.description: Unique identifier of the specimen within the study, assigned by the data provider.
fields.name: submitter_specimen_id

fields.description:
Indicate the tissue source of the specimen from which a biopsy or other tissue specimen was obtained using codes from NCIt (NCI Thesaurus).
Provide code in Compact URI (CURIE) pattern.
fields.name: specimen_tissue_source_code

fields.description: Provide the standardized and human readable term derived from the coding system associated with the specimen tissue source code.
fields.name: specimen_tissue_source_term

fields.description: Indicate the method of long term storage for specimen that were not extracted freshly or immediately cultured.
fields.name: specimen_storage

fields.description: Indicate the technique used to process specimen.
fields.name: specimen_processing

codeList: Cryopreservation of live cells in liquid nitrogen

fields.description: Indicate participant's age( in days) when specimen was collected.
fields.name: age_at_specimen_collection

fields.description:
Indicate the ICD-O-3 topography code or UBERON code for the anatomic location of a specimen when it was collected. Please use C80.9 if the anatomic location of a specimen is Unkown.
Provide code in Compact URI (CURIE) pattern.
Refer to the International Classification of Diseases for Oncology, 3rd Edition (WHO ICD-O-3) manual for guidelines at https://apps.who.int/iris/handle/10665/42344
UBERON URL: http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_{code}

required: Refer to the International Classification of Diseases for Oncology, 3rd Edition (WHO ICD-O-3) manual for guidelines at https://apps.who.int/iris/handle/10665/42344.

fields.name: specimen_anatomic_location_code

fields.description: Provide the standardized and human readable term derived from the coding system associated with the specimen anatomic location code.
fields.name: specimen_anatomic_location_term

fields.description: For disease in a paired organ, indicate the side on which the specimen was obtained.
fields.name: specimen_laterality

schemas.name: specimen

schemas.description: Refers to the molecular material (e.g., DNA, RNA, protein) extracted from the specimen and used for experiments or analysis.

fields.description: Unique identifier of the sample within the study, assigned by the data provider.
fields.name: submitter_sample_id

fields.description: Unique identifier of the specimen within the study, assigned by the data provider.
fields.name: submitter_specimen_id

fields.description:
Indicate NCIT or SO code to denote the type of source material used for sequencing and analysis.
Provide code in Compact URI (CURIE) pattern.
fields.name: molecule_type_code

fields.description: Provide the standardized and human readable term derived from the coding system associated with molecule type code.
fields.name: molecule_type_term

fields.description:
Indicate the status of the sample for data analysis.
Case sample refers to a sample that meets the disease or exposure criteria under investigation.
Control sample refers to of the same type that does not meet the criteria, representing a normal or baseline state for comparison.
fields.name: sample_status

schemas.name: sample

schemas.description: Contains information about the experimental design of the sequencing

fields.description: Unique identifier of the experiment within the study, assigned by the data provider.
fields.name: submitter_experiment_id

fields.description: Unique identifier of the sample within the study, assigned by the data provider.
fields.name: submitter_sample_id

fields.description: Indicate NCIt (NCI Thesaurus) code and term to denote the broad type of sequencing experiment performed. A mixture of library strategy and source.
fields.name: experiment_type

fields.description: The high-level experiment design including layout, protocol.
fields.name: experiment_design

fields.description:
Indicate OBI code to provide the sequencing technique intended for the library for sequencing data.
Provide code in Compact URI (CURIE) pattern.
fields.name: assay_type_code

fields.description: Provide the standardized and human readable term derived from the coding system associated with the assay type code.
fields.name: assay_type_term

fields.description: The sequencing platform type used in data generation.
fields.name: platform

fields.description:
Provide detailed information about the sequencing platform or technology used, including the name and/or model number of the specific instrument. Whenever possible, specify the exact model or revision rather than the general manufacturer name. It is recommended to use standardized terminology by including the appropriate OBI (Ontology for Biomedical Investigations) code and term to ensure precision.

Use the format: OBI code in CURIE format (OBI term)
fields.name: instrument

fields.description: Captures metadata about sequencing instrument usage (e.g. instruments parameters and usage conditions)
fields.name: instrument_metadata

fields.description:
Set of rules which guides how the sequencing protocol was followed. Change-tracking services such as Protocol.io or GitHub are encouraged instead of dumping free text in this field.
E.g, https://www.protocols.io/view/environmental-dna-edna-metabarcoding-protocol-for-rm7vzy3q2lx1/v1
fields.name: sequencing_protocol

schemas.name: experiment

schemas.description: Holds the readgroup information about the raw reads generated in a run of sequencing

fields.description: The identifier of a read group; must be unique within each payload.
fields.name: submitter_read_group_id

fields.description: Unique identifier of the experiment within the study, assigned by the data provider.
fields.name: submitter_experiment_id

fields.description: Name of the sequencing file containing reads from the first end of a sequencing run.
fields.name: file_r1

fields.description: Name of the sequencing file containing reads from the second end of a paired-end sequencing run. Required if and only if paired-end sequencing was done.
fields.name: file_r2

fields.description: Name of a sequencing library made from a molecular sample or a sample pool (multiplex sequencing).
fields.name: library_name

fields.description: Indicate whether the library was built as paired-end or single-end if applicable.
fields.name: library_layout

fields.description: Unique identifier for each read group. For example, Illumina has format {FLOWCELL_BARCODE}.{LANE}.{SAMPLE_BARCODE}. The {FLOWCELL_BARCODE} refers to the unique identifier for a particular flow cell. The {LANE} indicates the lane of the flow cell and the {SAMPLE_BARCODE} is a sample/library-specific identifier. For non-multiplex sequencing, platform unit and read group have a one-to-one relationship.
fields.name: platform_unit

fields.description: Description of the nucleotide sequencing library, including targeting information, spot, gap descriptors, and any other information relevant to its construction
fields.name: library_description

fields.description: Optional field indicating the @RD ID in the BAM. If submitted, this will be used to map the @RG ID in the BAM header to the submitter_read_group_id in the payload. After submission, the @RG ID in the payload will be used for all future headers. This cannot be submitted for FASTQ files.
fields.name: read_group_id_in_bam

fields.description: Length of sequencing reads in file_r1; this corresponds to the number of sequencing cycles of the first end.
fields.name: read_length_r1

fields.description: Length of sequencing reads in file_r2; this corresponds to the number of sequencing cycles of the second end.
fields.name: read_length_r2

fields.description: For paired-end sequencing, the average size of sequences between two sequencing ends. Required only for paired-end sequencing.
fields.name: insert_size

schemas.name: read_group

schemas.description: This entity is intended to contain metadata about a detailed examination of data (mainly data processing protocols) in order to come to some conclusion. It can be of different types (e.g. sequence variation, sequence alignment, phenotype characterization, gene expression, etc.) that will mainly differ in the protocols used to achieve the processed data of the analysis.

fields.description: Unique identifier of the study.
fields.name: studyId

fields.description: Unique identifier of the analysis within the study, assigned by the data provider.
fields.name: submitter_analysis_id

fields.description: Overall type of the analysis. Term chosen from a controlled vocabulary (CV) list.
fields.name: analysisType

fields.description: Unique identifier of the participant within the study, assigned by the data provider.
fields.name: submitter_participant_id

fields.description: Unique identifier of the specimen within the study, assigned by the data provider.
fields.name: submitter_specimen_id

fields.description: Unique identifier of the sample within the study, assigned by the data provider.
fields.name: submitter_sample_id

fields.description: Unique identifier of the experiment within the study, assigned by the data provider.
fields.name: submitter_experiment_id

fields.description: Indicate the category of data produced as outputs from the analysis.
fields.name: data_category

fields.description: Indicates whether the called variants are germline, somatic, or of uncertain classification.
fields.name: variant_class

fields.description: Indicate the approach used to identify genetic variants from sequencing data, based on the availability of reference samples (e.g., matched normal) and the study design.
fields.name: variant_calling_strategy

fields.description: The reference genome build that is used in the workflow.
fields.name: genome_build

fields.description: Indicates which genome annotation build or version was used in the analysis (e.g., Ensembl release, RefSeq release, etc.).
fields.name: genome_annotation

schemas.name: analysis

schemas.description: Contains information about the analysis workflow generating the analysis

fields.description: Indicate the unique identifier of the data processing workflow used in the analysis.
fields.name: submitter_workflow_id

fields.description: Unique identifier of the analysis within the study, assigned by the data provider.
fields.name: submitter_analysis_id

fields.description: The name of the genomic data processing workflow used for data analysis.
fields.name: workflow_name

fields.description: The version of the workflow or pipeline being used.
fields.name: workflow_version

fields.description: A URL linking to the workflow's repository or documentation. This could be a reference to a GitHub repository, a website where the workflow is described, or documentation on how to run the workflow.
fields.name: workflow_url

schemas.name: workflow

schemas.description: Contains information about the files generated from a run of sequencing or data analysis

fields.description: Unique identifier of the analysis within the study, assigned by the data provider.
fields.name: submitter_analysis_id

fields.description: File name, subdirectory-relative path, or absolute path of the data file. For more details see https://github.com/Pan-Canadian-Genome-Library/molecular-data-submission-workflow/blob/main/docs/input.md.
fields.name: fileName

fields.description: Size of the file, in bytes. If not provided, will be populated by molecular submission workflow.
fields.name: fileSize

fields.description: Computed md5sum of the file. If not provided, will be populated by molecular submission workflow.
fields.name: fileMd5sum

fields.description: Data format of files.
fields.name: fileType

fields.description: The permission level of a file for public access.
fields.name: fileAccess

fields.description: Indicates the type of data produced as outputs from the analysis.
fields.name: dataType

schemas.name: file
